column1: Plain English ≠ ML in general (only LLMs) why?     Here i have confused between LLM's and natural language interface, Machine learning is nothing but human provides the data and machine learns the rules, in traditional programming - human provides the rules and machine executes them.

Wrong interpretation of supervised and unsupervised learning.       Supervised learning is like data will have labels, ex: 1000 emails, each tagged spam/not-spam Unsupervised learning - data does not have labels, ex: having 1000 customers data, and grouping them into certain types without telling that group exists.

Train/test split    Here, Train data which is used to test the data, test data is something which can not be seen during the training, But when you give the test data, real working behavior of model comes out.

What is Overfitting?    Over fitting means, while you training the model, model remembers/memorizes the training data instead of learning the patterns so when you test with test data, the model fails.
